curator_log.rescue_path: 1 (restore legacy.bak: test.sh + test_manifest.yaml from .legacy.bak) + 3 (overturn prior DROP — instruction.md is verbatim FIRST user turn only; the 7 subsequent user-sim turns carry the substantive deliverables that the legacy verifier scores).
curator_log.drop_reason_overturned: Prior DROP claimed TEST_DIFFERENT_TARGET because instruction.md says 'read plans/agent-session-refactor.md' and that file does not exist at base commit a1e1078. Two errors. (1) Per claudeMd, instruction.md is verbatim the FIRST user turn ONLY -- it is not the entire task. The session has 8 user turns (verified in user_simulation_prompt.md): T1 'read plans/agent-session-refactor.md', T2 'ok, do it', T3 'should go into suit, no?', T4 'well move it', T5 'does this use faux provider?', T6 'update the .md file accordingly', T7 'put regression tests under suit/regressions/issue-number-description-text.test.ts?', T8 'can you encode that in README.md in test/suite? and also agents.md in the root dir, concisely'. The user-sim conditions are observable file-state predicates (e.g., 'Agent has created a test file at packages/coding-agent/test/suite/agent-session-queue.test.ts'), so these turns fire reactively even if the agent never reads the missing plan file. (2) The patch_library shows 4 of 5 cohorts (deepseek_v4_pro 8 ops, deepseek_v4_flash 26 ops, minimax25 15 ops, minimax27 3 ops; opus46 only 1 op via plan-write) actually performed the substantive work: writing test/suite/agent-session-queue.test.ts using harness/faux provider, adding the pi-tui alias to packages/coding-agent/vitest.config.ts, creating regressions/<issue>-<slug>.test.ts files, editing AGENTS.md and test/suite/README.md. This IS the canonical deliverable -- not 'read a plan file'. (3) The active tests/test.sh is a SWE-rebench inert template (84 lines, runs `bun x vitest run` globally with FAIL_TO_PASS=[] and falls back to overall pass rate -- a no-op patch could score >0 simply because the base repo's existing test suite passes). The purpose-built verifier was clobbered to .legacy.bak. fix_summary.md confirms the legacy verifier was tuned with Sonnet 0.90 vs Haiku 0.40 (gap 0.50, well above 0.15 threshold). Mirrors the 0467b78e rescue pattern verbatim.
fixes_applied: Restored tests/test.sh from tests/test.sh.legacy.bak (3364 -> 13682 bytes, replaces SWE-rebench inert stub with the 346-line purpose-built verifier scoring: F2P1 vitest behavioral on test/suite/ harness tests = 0.45, F2P2 vitest.config.ts pi-tui alias = 0.20, F2P3 plan updates faux+suite = 0.15, F2P4 docs suite README + AGENTS.md = 0.12, F2P5 regressions/ convention = 0.08, plus upstream gates f2p_upstream_vitest_queue_suite (0.20) and f2p_upstream_vitest_prompt_suite (0.20) using weighted-replace formula, plus informational P2P_REGRESSION p2p_upstream_tsc_coding_agent per v0.4.3.1 standard).

Restored tests/test_manifest.yaml from tests/test_manifest.yaml.legacy.bak (file was missing in active task; legacy backup declares 18 gates: 13 inline F2P weighted gates summing to 0.6, plus f2p_upstream_vitest_queue_suite=0.20 + f2p_upstream_vitest_prompt_suite=0.20 = 0.40 upstream weight, plus 2 P2P_GATING gates and 3 P2P_REGRESSION informational gates).
curator_log.notes: Known minor gap unrelated to the rescue: fix_summary.md documents that the original Dockerfile COPYed a reconstructed plans/agent-session-refactor.md into the image (the file did not exist at base commit a1e1078). The current Dockerfile is a thin child off ghcr.io/togetherbench/togetherbench/pi-mono-dev:latest (auto-generated by scripts/rewrite_task_dockerfiles.py) and dropped both the COPY directive and the source plan file. Effect on scoring: the agent can still complete 7 of 8 turns without reading the missing plan (sim turns fire on observable file-state, not on whether the plan was read). F2P3 'plan updates' awards up to 0.15 if the agent CREATES the plan with faux+suite content (which models do: opus46's only op IS writing this file; deepseek_v4_flash also writes it). So even the missing plan file does not block the scoring path -- the agent recreates it as part of T6. Worst case ~0.15 ceiling-shave for models that skip T6, but the legacy verifier is robust to plan absence. CI lint flags a known R006 (single dominant F2P gate) but the dominant gate is the inline F2P1 (vitest behavioral, weight 0.45 within the inner reward), well-bounded by the upstream weighted-replace formula at the tail. Recommend a Dockerfile follow-up to reinstate the plan COPY, but not a blocker for KEEP. Test sum: 0.6 inline + 0.4 upstream = 1.0, within manifest cap.

diffs.tests/test.sh: restored from tests/test.sh.legacy.bak (84 -> 346 lines, replaces SWE-rebench inert stub `bun x vitest run` with purpose-built verifier scoring suite/harness tests + pi-tui alias + plan/AGENTS docs + regressions convention)
diffs.tests/test_manifest.yaml: created from tests/test_manifest.yaml.legacy.bak (file was missing entirely in the active task; legacy backup contains the canonical 18-gate declaration)
